Scenario.title: APT41 DUST Attack on Water Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A coordinated cyber attack campaign by APT41 DUST group targets water and wastewater facilities, employing advanced multi-stage tactics from initial reconnaissance through to operational impact, leveraging expert-level skills and existing vulnerabilities in industrial control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Script, Internet-facing assets, Command, File, Internet Scan, OS Process, User Account, File Modification, Network Traffic, Logon Session, Malware Artifact
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Script Execution (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Command Execution (Mechanism), File Deletion (Mechanism), Response Content (Mechanism), Process Injection, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Encrypted Channel, Manipulation of Control

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers utilize artificial intelligence tools to gather and analyze publicly available data on the water facility, including employee details, vendor relationships, and system documentation, thereby identifying potential vulnerabilities and entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The group develops specialized scripts to automate the collection of information on SCADA and ICS vulnerabilities, tailoring malware to exploit common issues in water treatment environments while evading standard detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers launch spearphishing campaigns by sending targeted emails impersonating regulatory bodies or utility providers, embedding malicious links to compromise credentials and achieve initial access to the facility's systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Malicious commands are injected and executed on vulnerable industrial controllers, allowing the attackers to manipulate real-time operational parameters such as pump settings or chemical dosing directly within the control environment.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: After gaining access, attackers configure persistent mechanisms by deleting or modifying key files to ensure automatic reinfection upon system reboots, maintaining long-term presence despite routine maintenance or incident response efforts.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Elevated privileges are achieved by manipulating client configurations or exploiting weak passwords on default accounts, scanning for high-privilege
